messages.content:
Which statement is NOT true for a healthy forest?
Group of answer choices

Human can impact the forest health by bringing exotic pathogens

The special scale can influence the evaluation criteria

Canker disease often happens in the old trees

There are different criteria from different aspect

None is correct.
None is correct.

Which is NOT true for rust?
Group of answer choices

None is correct.

Heteroecious rusts must alternate on different hosts

Domestic rust can be prevented by genetic method

Exotic rust cannot be devastating

Rust is one of the most destructive group of plant pathogens
Exotic rust cannot be devastating is NOT true.

Which statement is NOT true for fungi?
Group of answer choices

Fungi participates in carbon and nutrient cycling

Fungi can be the food for wildlife

It is easier for beetle to reproduce on fungi

Fungi can decompose cellulose but not lignin

None is correct.
Fungi can decompose cellulose but not lignin is NOT true.

Which of the following statement is correct?
Group of answer choices

Landsat datasets should be avoided to examine the historical conditions of landscape changes due to its low temporal resolution.

Both MODIS and Landsat can be moderate scale resolution imagery in terms of their spectral coverage.

Landsat imagery may not be ideal for studying changes in daily to weekly vegetation conditions.

Landsat imagery can help determine the tree height and branch numbers accurately.

No option is correct.

Neither Landsat or MODIS can be used to monitor the age structure of forests.

Unlike MODIS data, Landsat imagery cannot be used to study forest fires.
Landsat datasets should be avoided to examine the historical conditions of landscape changes due to its low temporal resolution. is NOT correct.

Which of the following statement is correct?
Group of answer choices

Landsat datasets should be avoided to examine the historical conditions of landscape changes due to its low temporal resolution.

Both MODIS and Landsat can be moderate scale resolution imagery in terms of their spectral coverage.

Landsat imagery may not be ideal for studying changes in daily to weekly vegetation conditions.

Landsat imagery can help determine the tree height and branch numbers accurately.

No option is correct.

Neither Landsat or MODIS can be used to monitor the age structure of forests.

Unlike MODIS data, Landsat imagery cannot be used to study forest fires.
Both MODIS and Landsat can be moderate scale resolution imagery in terms of their spectral coverage.

Which of the following may NOT be used to differentiate between remote sensing systems and the data they collect?
Group of answer choices

All options can be used to differentiate remote sensing systems and their data collection.

Sources of energy (e.g. passive vs. active)

Radiometric resolution

Platform type (e.g. ground-based, vs., airborne, vs. spaceborne)

Spatial resolution

Swath
All options can be used to differentiate remote sensing systems and their data collection.

Which remote sensing data type would be best for locating flooded areas underneath a forest canopy?
Group of answer choices

RADAR

Both Landsat and MODIS

LiDAR

Google Earth

UAV

Landsat

MERIS

MODIS
LiDAR would be best for locating flooded areas underneath a forest canopy as it can penetrate through the vegetation canopy and measure the height of the surfaces beneath it.

Which remote sensing data type would be best for locating flooded areas underneath a forest canopy?
Group of answer choices

RADAR

Both Landsat and MODIS

LiDAR

Google Earth

UAV

Landsat

MERIS

MODIS
LiDAR would be best for locating flooded areas underneath a forest canopy as it can penetrate through the vegetation canopy and measure the height of the surfaces beneath it.

What remote sensing dataset would be most appropriate for studying the seasonal patterns of vegetation for the whole planet over a single year?
Group of answer choices

Both Landsat and IKONOS

Landsat

MODIS

LiDAR

IKONOS

RADAR

MERIS

Google Earth

MODIS would be most appropriate for studying the seasonal patterns of vegetation for the whole planet over a single year. MODIS has a high temporal resolution, capturing data every 1-2 days and can provide a continuous view of vegetation changes over the entire globe.

Which of the following statement regarding modelling approaches is wrong?
Group of answer choices

Empirical simulators need the past growth data at the tree and stand level.

Mechanistic simulators consider the impacts of biological processes on forest growth and yield.

All options are correct.

Single tree simulators require tree-level data input for forest modelling.

Single tree simulators need spatial locations of individual trees in certain cases.

Whole stand simulators need both input and output stand level variables.
All options are correct.

Which of the following may NOT be used as one of the stand attributes (e.g. structural characteristics) for old-growth forests?
Group of answer choices

Variation of tree ages, sizes, and spacing

Growth rate of dominant trees

Accumulation of large, dead, standing, and fallen trees

Multiple canopy layers

Understory patchiness and canopy gaps

All factors are correct.
All factors are correct.

Which of the following may NOT be used as one of the stand attributes (e.g. structural characteristics) for old-growth forests?
Group of answer choices

Variation of tree ages, sizes, and spacing

Growth rate of dominant trees

Accumulation of large, dead, standing, and fallen trees

Multiple canopy layers

Understory patchiness and canopy gaps
All factors are correct.

The amount of timber produced by a specific forest stand over a certain period of time can be defined as 
 .

The increase in certain tree attributes over a certain period of time can be defined as 
 .

The amount of timber produced by a specific forest stand over a certain period of time can be defined as yield. 

The increase in certain tree attributes over a certain period of time can be defined as growth.
